title: Event-Driven Wake Scheduler
non_product_work: Resolve the bootstrap gap where Bandit still depends on recurring LLM polling to discover available work and lacks a repo-native Work Availability Wake Guarantee.
origin: The PRD-002 research review found that recurring LLM polling heartbeats waste tokens on no-op discovery. The operator accepted event-driven scheduling only if work still wakes reliably when available. `BANDIT-GAP-EVENT-DRIVEN-WAKE-SCHEDULER` is now the first queued bootstrap gap after `BANDIT-051` resolved the Worktree Bootstrap Contract.
scope: Define event-driven work triggers for newly claimable work, recovery-required work, newly unblocked in-progress work, and operator-resolved blockers without treating the trigger itself as workflow authority.
Define a deterministic non-LLM sweeper for stale claims, missed triggers, due evaluations, blocker-state changes, and clean no-op discovery.
Prove a Work Availability Wake Guarantee before removing fallback LLM polling behavior for ordinary work discovery.
Define a single-claim activation contract that selects at most one runnable stage after deterministic reconciliation.
Define scheduler priority ordering: recovery-required claims first, continuable active work next, highest-priority unblocked queued work next, then eligible low-risk chores.
Use roadmap order, backlog order, and work item ID as deterministic tie-breakers when priority is otherwise equal.
Require scheduler and sweeper output to explain selected work, skipped work, blocked work, ineligible work, and no-op decisions.

Preserve the Claim Authority, Git Mutation Serializer, Worktree Bootstrap Contract, Work-Surface Wait-For Graph, Coordination Event Log Authority, Operator Fail-Closed Boundary, Input Quarantine Gate, Layered Risk Classification, Supply-Chain Gate, and review-subject-hash boundaries.
Record CLEAN_CODE.md read evidence in Stage 1; CLEAN_CODE.md was read on 2026-05-29 before creating this brief, and clean-code compliance must be evaluated before landing.

Stage capability scope: Codex PM owns Stage 1 brief creation, Stage 2 RED evidence, routine technical routing, and context-artifact synchronization; if Codex authors or materially edits Stage 2 RED tests, Stage 3 implementation is assigned to Claude through the bootstrap Process Adapter path; the Stage 3 Writer has no authority to edit tests, test helpers, fixtures, RED evidence, or acceptance mappings; CodeRabbit and Local Qwen own Stage 4 review evidence; Landing Agent owns Stage 5 verdict/action evidence.

Token-cost failsafe boundary: the scheduler contract must reduce no-op LLM wakeups and define generous abnormal-run failsafes for repeated wake/no-op patterns, but it must not approve paid, high-token, recurring reviewer, or broader cost-policy changes; `BANDIT-GAP-TOKEN-COST-FAILSAFE` remains queued for the full provider-pricing and spend-approval contract.

Future-work scope: this chore must not implement full scheduler execution, batch queue draining, multi-repo scheduling, full worktree lifecycle enablement, true parallel writable workstream enablement, claim lease creation or release, claim authority changes, work-surface reservation implementation, automatic merge/push/deploy behavior, product UAT approval, cockpit UI/server/API work, state-index persistence, actor identity policy, PR/CI workflow execution, live reviewer routing changes, paid reviewer routes, external service integration, installed global skill edits, dependency or lockfile changes, or unrelated Phase 8 work.
acceptance_criteria: The chore brief exists at docs/work/<ID>/brief.md and links to BANDIT-GAP-EVENT-DRIVEN-WAKE-SCHEDULER.

Stage 1 brief evidence records CLEAN_CODE.md read evidence, Stage capability scope, Codex-owned technical decisions, source hierarchy, event-driven trigger boundary, deterministic sweeper boundary, Work Availability Wake Guarantee, single-claim activation boundary, token-cost failsafe boundary, and no-authority trigger boundary.

Focused RED evidence proves Bandit currently lacks enforced event-driven wake triggers, deterministic non-LLM sweep behavior, and a Work Availability Wake Guarantee before LLM polling can be removed for ordinary work discovery.
Newly claimable work, recovery-required work, newly unblocked in-progress work, and operator-resolved blockers wake the appropriate scheduler or agent path.
The deterministic sweeper can recover missed triggers and detect stale claims, due evaluations, and blocker-state changes without LLM execution.
No runnable work produces deterministic no-op output without waking an LLM just to discover ordinary no-op state.
A woken scheduler activation selects at most one claimable stage and does not batch-drain the queue.
Scheduler output explains why selected work was chosen and why blocked, overlapping, stale, ambiguous, or ineligible work was skipped.
Eligible low-risk chores are selected only when no recovery-required claim, continuable active work, or higher-priority unblocked queued work exists.
Ambiguous, unreconciled, or conflicting repo state fails closed instead of guessing from chat, dashboard state, generated projections, or trigger text.

Event triggers, sweeper output, cockpit views, and generated projections do not become canonical workflow authority; append-only coordination history, claim authority, roadmap/current-context, and work-item artifacts remain source of truth according to their existing boundaries.
Fallback polling stays available until event-driven triggers and deterministic sweeping prove that available work wakes reliably.

Repeated wake/no-op patterns, scheduler retries, and abnormal long-running scheduler behavior have a recorded failsafe path, without turning the broader queued token-cost policy gap into scope for this chore.
Stage 4 review evidence uses pre-PR CodeRabbit and Local Qwen at the current review subject hash unless honest provider refusal or bootstrap-gap evidence is recorded.

Layered risk-classification and supply-chain gate evidence are recorded before landing because this chore touches workflow scheduling, policy validation, generated schemas or templates, command routing, and potential executable trigger surfaces.
Clean-code compliance is evaluated before landing; any accepted non-blocking concern becomes a tagged follow-up or explicit no-action decision.
BANDIT-GAP-EVENT-DRIVEN-WAKE-SCHEDULER is resolved or explicitly dispositioned in .bandit/bootstrap-gaps.json only after landing action and retrospective closeout evidence exist.

No full scheduler execution, batch queue draining, multi-repo scheduling, full worktree lifecycle enablement, true parallel writable workstream enablement, claim lease creation or release, claim authority change, work-surface reservation implementation, automatic merge/push/deploy behavior, product UAT approval, cockpit UI/server/API work, state-index persistence, actor identity policy, PR/CI workflow execution, live reviewer routing change, paid reviewer route, external service integration, installed global skill edit, dependency or lockfile change, or unrelated Phase 8 work is introduced.
verification_plan: Run focused Event-Driven Wake Scheduler RED/GREEN tests for newly claimable work, recovery-required work, newly unblocked in-progress work, operator-resolved blockers, deterministic sweeper recovery, missed triggers, stale claims, due evaluations, blocker-state changes, clean no-op output, ambiguous-state refusal, and one-claim activation behavior.

Run focused priority tests proving recovery-required claims outrank continuable active work, continuable active work outranks newly unblocked queued work, queued work outranks eligible low-risk chores, and deterministic tie-breakers are stable.
Run focused tests proving event triggers and sweeper output do not become workflow authority and cannot bypass claim authority, coordination history, roadmap/current-context, or work-item artifacts.
Run focused tests proving fallback polling is not removed until the Work Availability Wake Guarantee passes.
Run focused tests proving repeated wake/no-op patterns and abnormal scheduler retries trip the recorded failsafe path without requiring a broader provider-pricing or spend-approval implementation.
Run node --test test/event-driven-wake-scheduler.test.mjs.
Run node --test test/heartbeat.test.mjs if heartbeat inspection or activation behavior is touched.
Run node --test test/coordination-status.test.mjs if coordination status or candidate projection behavior is touched.
Run node --test test/cockpit-status.test.mjs if derived cockpit status exposes wake scheduler or sweeper state.
Run node --test test/work-item-create.test.mjs if work-item brief rendering or parsing is touched.
Run node --test test/validate.test.mjs if repo validation behavior is touched.

Run npm test if implementation touches shared command routing, validators, artifact renderers, work item parsing, templates, bootstrap gaps, risk classification, supply-chain gates, claim authority, git mutation policy, cockpit status, coordination status, heartbeat behavior, or policy validation beyond focused tests.
Run npm run typecheck.
Run npm run bandit -- validate.
Run npm run bandit -- gaps list.
Run node ./bin/bandit.mjs cockpit status --json.
Run node ./bin/bandit.mjs session-context current --json.
Run npm run bandit -- coordination-authority validate --json.
Run npm run bandit -- claim validate --json if claim authority, claim state, fencing token, or claim projection behavior is touched.
Run npm run bandit -- git-mutation validate --json if worktree-related shared `.git` mutation policy is touched.
Run npm run bandit -- supply-chain-gate validate --json because scheduler triggers, generated schemas or templates, command routing, and executable hook surfaces can affect supply-chain posture.
Run npm run bandit -- risk-classification validate --json.
Run npm run bandit -- input-quarantine validate --json.
Run npm run bandit -- operator-boundary validate --json.
Run node ./bin/bandit.mjs review-subject-hash <ID> for aggregate review evidence freshness.
Run npm run bandit -- coderabbit-review pre-pr <ID> --base origin/main before Stage 4 closeout, unless provider refusal evidence is recorded.
Run npm run bandit -- qwen-review <ID> before Stage 4 closeout.
Run npm run bandit -- land-check <ID> before landing.
Run git diff --check.
operator_input_status: No operator-owned input is required before creating this bootstrap-gap chore or writing RED evidence. Repo artifacts identify the queued gap, source artifacts, accepted event-driven scheduling condition, current Stage Rubric requirements, Clean-Code authority, Claim Authority boundary, Git Mutation Serializer boundary, Worktree Bootstrap Contract boundary, Operator Fail-Closed Boundary, and Codex PM boundary that scheduler wake mechanics are routine technical decisions when repo evidence and policy are sufficient. Halt only if implementation would change product direction, UAT policy, workflow policy beyond enforcing the accepted event-driven wake scheduler gap, business tradeoffs, explicit cost/risk posture, external service setup, paid reviewer spend approval, paid reviewer routing, recurring cost policy, live routing, claim authority, worktree lifecycle authority, installed global skill contents, dependency or lockfile policy, merge/push/deploy authority, or broader workflow scope.
